Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 2, 173 [Udug-hul Compendium][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oآ 1oآ 1

dnam-tar udug gal irigal-la kur â¸¢nuâ¸£-giâ‚„-giâ‚„-keâ‚„

(o 1) Namtar (Akkadian: ditto), the powerful demon of the grave, (the demon) of the land of no return!

dMIN<(NAM.TAR)> uâ‚‚-tuk-ku GALu إ،aâ‚‚ qab-ri â¸¢er-ل¹£eâ¸£-ti la ta-ri
oآ 2oآ 2

dnam-tar luâ‚‚kaإ،â‚„!(KASâ‚…) diإ‹ir-re-e-ne-keâ‚„

(o 2) Namtar (Akkadian: ditto), courier of the gods, Namtar (Akkadian: ditto), who afflicts the restless man (Akkadian: by whom the restless man is afflicted), nobody knows his ways.

dMIN<(NAM.TAR)> la-si-im DINGIR-MEإ 
oآ 3oآ 3

dnam-tar luâ‚‚ paâ‚„-ل¸«al إ‹ar-ra-a a-raâ‚‚-bi luâ‚‚-na-me nu-zu

dMIN<(NAM.TAR)> a-me-<lu> mut-tal-lik iإ،â‚‚-إ،aâ‚‚-kinâ‚‚-ma a-lak-ta-إ،uâ‚‚ mam-ma ul i-de1
oآ 4oآ 4

إ،agâ‚„ luâ‚‚ tu-ra-keâ‚„ luâ‚‚-na-me nu-zu

(o 4) Nobody knows the interior of the ill one (Akkadian: his illness); nobody sees the poison of the ill one (Akkadian: the poison of his illness that is invisible).

qeâ‚‚-reb mur-â¸¢ل¹£iâ¸£-إ،uâ‚‚ mam-ma ul i-de
oآ 5oآ 5

uإ،â‚پâ‚پ luâ‚‚ tu-ra-â¸¢keâ‚„!?â¸£ luâ‚‚ igi nu-un-bar-ra

i-mat mur-â¸¢ل¹£i-إ،uâ‚‚?â¸£ إ،aâ‚‚ la nap-lu-su
oآ 66

dasar-luâ‚‚-[ل¸«i] igi <im>-ma-an-sigâ‚پâ‚€ : niإ‹â‚‚ إ‹aâ‚‚-e : إ‹en-na dumu-إ‹uâ‚پâ‚€2

(o 6) Asarluhi sees (...) : the thing I (...) : go my child!

oآ 77

muإ،en an-na-â¸¢keâ‚„?â¸£ tumâ‚پâ‚‚ an-â¸¢na-keâ‚„â¸£ : iل¹£-ل¹£ur ANe si-â¸¢matâ¸£ ANe

(o 7) The bird of heavens, the dove of heavens (is) the exorcist, the diviner of Enki (Akkadian: Ea).

oآ 8oآ 8

maإ،-maإ، maإ،â‚‚-إ،u-gidâ‚‚-gidâ‚‚ den-ki-ga-keâ‚„

a-إ،i-pu ba-ru-u deâ‚‚-a
oآ 9oآ 9

إ‹eإ،eren uâ‚‚â¸¢إ،imâ¸£li an-na-keâ‚„ inim bala-bala-e

(o 9) "Cedar and burؤپإ،u-juniper of An", (those) are your words.

ere-nu bu-ra-إ،uâ‚‚ إ،aâ‚‚ da-nim at-mu-ka
oآ 10oآ 10

muإ،en an-na-keâ‚„ tumâ‚پâ‚‚ â¸¢muâ¸£-un-إ،i-in-bar-ra

(o 10) The bird of heavens, the dove, is released (Akkadian: and) if you let (it) fly (Akkadian: it flies) at sunrise (but) you do not let (it) fly (Akkadian: it does not fly) at sunset.

iل¹£-ل¹£ur ANe â¸¢siâ¸£-ma-tuâ‚‚ muإ،-إ،ir-ma
oآ 11oآ 11

tukumbi â¸¢igiâ¸£ dutu-eâ‚ƒ-إ،eâ‚ƒ iâ‚ƒ-dal-en igi dutu-إ،uâ‚‚-a-إ،eâ‚ƒ nu-iâ‚ƒ-dal-en

إ،um-ma ana ل¹£i-it dUTUإ،i â¸¢itâ¸£-tap-riإ، ana pa-an e-reb dUTUإ،i la i-tap-riإ،
oآ 1212

[إ،u sagâ‚‰]-â¸¢ga diإ‹irâ¸£-[ra-na-إ،eâ‚ƒ] ل¸«eâ‚‚-en-إ،i-in-gi-gi3

(o 12) May (the sick one) return to his god's favourable hand.

(o 17) [...] gives (Akkadian: the man) poison to drink;

(o 19) [...] decreed a fate for them (with which) they do not agree

[...] â¸¢إ،imâ¸£-ti i-â¸¢إ،im-إ،uâ‚‚-nu-teâ¸£
Reverse
rآ 1rآ 1

[...] teإ،â‚‚ nu-tuku su-bi-إ،eâ‚ƒ x [...]

(r 1) [...] he is fearless in his body [...]

(r 2) (in) their leaving [...] they will carry out an attack against the land

[...] â¸¢a-ل¹£e-إ،uâ‚‚â¸£-[nu]-te ana ma-a-tuâ‚‚ ti-ba-a â¸¢iإ،-tak-nuâ¸£
rآ 3rآ 3

[...]-ginâ‚‡ kalam-ma mu-un-siâ‚ƒ

(r 3) [...] they will fill the land [...] (Akkadian: with locusts)

[...] e-ra-beâ‚‚-e KUR im-ta-lu-u
rآ 4rآ 4

[... teإ‹â‚ƒ]-إ‹eâ‚‚â‚†-â¸¢da?â¸£ aâ‚‚-sagâ‚ƒ gig-ga ba-ni-in-إ‹ar-eإ،

(r 4) [...] will approach [...] (Akkadian: the man and) he will be afflicted by a grievous asakku-demon

[...] â¸¢LUâ‚‚â¸£ iل¹­-ل¸«e-ma a-sak-ku mar-ل¹£u i-taإ،-kan
rآ 5rآ 5

[...] â¸¢uâ‚ˆâ¸£ a-â¸¢nirâ¸£-ra mu-un-guâ‚‡-â¸¢eâ¸£

[...] ta-neâ‚‚-ل¸«i e-kal
rآ 6rآ 6

rآ 7rآ 7

[...]-â¸¢RI?â¸£-إ،uâ‚‚ â¸¢imâ¸£-[ta-ل¹­uâ‚‚]-u6
rآ 88

[...] â¸¢:â¸£ niإ‹â‚‚ إ‹aâ‚‚-e : إ‹en-â¸¢naâ¸£ dumu-إ‹uâ‚پâ‚€7

(r 8) [...] : what I (...) : go my child!

rآ 99

â¸¢im abzu-ta u?-me?â¸£-[ni-kidâ‚‚] ل¹­i-ل¹­i ap-si-i gi-â¸¢riâ¸£-iل¹£-ma

(r 9) Break off the clay of the Abzu (Akkadian: then) fashion his substitute's figurine out of clay; fashion his counterpart's figurine out of clay (Akkadian: then) lay (it) on the patient's lap (during) the night.

rآ 10rآ 10

alan niإ‹â‚‚-saإ‹-ilâ‚‚-la-a-ni im u-me-ni-â¸¢dimâ‚‚â¸£

ل¹£a-lam di-â¸¢naâ¸£-ni-إ،uâ‚‚ إ،aâ‚‚ ل¹­i-ل¹­i bi-ni-ma8
rآ 11rآ 11

â¸¢alanâ¸£ [ki]-â¸¢biâ¸£-in-إ‹ar-a-ni im u-me-ni-â¸¢dimâ‚‚â¸£

ل¹£a-lam pu-â¸¢ل¸«iâ¸£-إ،uâ‚‚ â¸¢إ،aâ‚‚â¸£ ل¹­i-ل¹­i bi-ni-ma
rآ 12rآ 12

urâ‚‚ luâ‚‚ â¸¢tuâ¸£-ra-keâ‚„ إ‹iâ‚†-na u-me-ni-nuâ‚‚

ina su-â¸¢unâ¸£ mar-ل¹£i mu-إ،u [إ،u]-ni-il-ma
rآ 1313

aâ‚‚-<guâ‚‚>-zi-ga-ta an-â¸¢urâ‚‚â¸£ zalagâ‚‚-e-deâ‚ƒ : ina إ،e-ri ina â¸¢na-maâ¸£-ri9

(r 13) At dawn, when the horizon becomes bright, bring (Akkadian: it) his representative (lit. the one who is like him) out to the open country, in front of Utu, bring (it) out in front of Utu, king of heavens and earth.

rآ 14rآ 14

e-ne-bi-ginâ‚‡ igi dutu-إ،eâ‚ƒ eden-na u-me-ni-eâ‚ƒ

GIM إ،u-ma-tu ma-ل¸«ar dUTU ana ل¹£e-ri إ،u-ل¹£i-إ،u-ma
rآ 15rآ 15

igi dâ¸¢utuâ¸£ lugal an ki-a u-me-ni-eâ‚ƒ

ana ma-â¸¢ل¸«arâ¸£ dUTU LUGAL ANe KItiâ‚ƒ إ،u-ل¹£i-ma
rآ 1616

aâ‚‚-إ‹aâ‚‚-â¸¢laâ‚‚â¸£ keإ،â‚‚-â¸¢daâ¸£ u-me-ni-إ،umâ‚‚ : na-ruq-qu ra-kis-tuâ‚‚ i-din-إ،uâ‚‚-ma

(r 16) Give him a bound leather bag (Akkadian: then) put it at the roots of an aإ،ؤپgu-tree (Akkadian: then) place it in front of him, at sunrise.

rآ 1717

durâ‚‚ kiإ،iâ‚پâ‚†-إ،eâ‚ƒ u-me-ni-إ‹ar : â¸¢ina iإ،â¸£-di e-إ،e-gi إ،u-kun-ma

rآ 1818

igi-bi dutu-إ،uâ‚‚-a-إ،eâ‚ƒ u-me-ni-إ‹ar : pa-ni-إ،uâ‚‚ ana e-reb إ،am-إ،i إ،u-kun-ma

rآ 19rآ 19

â¸¢alanâ¸£-bi igi dutu-إ،eâ‚ƒ ki-bi-â¸¢inâ¸£-إ‹ar-ra-bi ل¸«eâ‚‚-a

(r 19) May he (place) his figurine, his counterpart, in front of Utu; may he (place) his figurine, his substitute, in front of Utu.

[ل¹£a]-lam-إ،u ma-ل¸«ar dUTU lu [pu]-ل¸«u-u-إ،uâ‚‚
rآ 20rآ 20

â¸¢alanâ¸£-bi igi dâ¸¢utuâ¸£-[إ،eâ‚ƒ] niإ‹â‚‚-saإ‹-ilâ‚‚-â¸¢laâ¸£-bi ل¸«eâ‚‚-a

[ل¹£a]-â¸¢lamâ¸£-إ،uâ‚‚ ma-ل¸«ar dUTU lu di-na-nu-إ،uâ‚‚
Left
l.e.آ 1l.e.آ 1

iâ‚ƒ saإ‹-إ‹aâ‚‚-na-إ،eâ‚ƒ u-me-ni-deâ‚‚

(l.e. 1) Pour oil on its (the substitute's) head.

â¸¢إ،amâ¸£-na ana qaq-qa-di-إ،uâ‚‚ إ،u-puk-ma10
Top
t.e.آ 1t.e.آ 1

[...] â¸¢udugâ¸£ ل¸«ul a-laâ‚‚ ل¸«ul bar-إ،eâ‚ƒ ل¸«eâ‚‚-em-ta-â¸¢gubâ¸£

(t.e. 1) May the evil udug-demon, the evil ala-demon stand outside (from him)!

t.e.آ 22

[...] â¸¢إ،eduâ‚‚â¸£ sagâ‚پâ‚€-ga dâ¸¢lammaâ¸£ [sagâ‚پâ‚€]-â¸¢gaâ¸£ ل¸«eâ‚‚-en-sugâ‚‚-sugâ‚‚-[ge-eإ،]

(t.e. 2) May the good إ،edu-spirit, the good lammar-deity assuredly stand (by him)!

t.e.آ 33

â¸¢enâ‚‚â¸£ dudug ل¸«ul-إ‹alâ‚‚-â¸¢eâ¸£ mu-un-duâ‚‡-[duâ‚‡]

(t.e. 3) Incantation: "The evil udug-demon keeps knocking (him)!".

1The Akkadian N-stem may also be a scribal mistake.

2No akkadian line (same on other versions).

3No Akkadian line (same on other versions).

4End of the first incantation.

5The equivalence between adؤپru and teإ،â‚‚ is not attested elsewhere.

6The verb should be D-stem.

7No Akkadian line (same in other versions).

8Lines 10 and 11 are inverted compared to other versions.

9One of the other versions is closer to the Sumerian as it bears: iإ،id إ،amأھ ina namؤپri. In the text we have here it is also possible to translate the Akkadian literally as follows: "in the morning, at dawn".

10These 2 lines are before r 17 in other versions.
